channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: ISIS Deputy Leader Killed in US-Nigeria Joint Operation
item.description: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the second-in-command of ISIS globally, was killed in a joint US-Nigerian military operation in northeastern Nigeria. President Trump and Nigerian President Tinubu confirmed the operation, marking a significant blow to the terrorist organization.

item.title: Tens of Thousands Protest in London as Rival Rallies Face Off
item.description: London deployed thousands of police officers to manage rival protests: a far-right "Unite the Kingdom" rally and a pro-Palestinian Nakba Day march. At least 11 people were arrested as authorities worked to keep the opposing groups separated.

item.title: IDF Infantry Commander Killed by Hezbollah Drone in Southern Lebanon
item.description: Captain Maoz Israel Recanati, 24, was killed by a Hezbollah drone attack in southern Lebanon. He was set to marry next month. The IDF struck over 100 targets in response.

item.title: Jerusalem Governorate Warns of Israeli Plan to Seize Properties Near Al-Aqsa
item.description: Palestinian authorities warn that Israel plans to approve seizure of Palestinian properties in the Bab al-Silsila neighborhood near Al-Aqsa Mosque on Sunday.

item.title: Trump Not Optimistic on Jimmy Lai Release After Raising Case With Xi
item.description: US President Trump says he raised the case of imprisoned Hong Kong media tycoon Jimmy Lai with Chinese President Xi, but is not optimistic about his release. Lai was sentenced to 20 years under Hong Kong national security law.

item.title: Hezbollah Urges Lebanon to Abandon Peace Illusions With Israel
item.description: Hezbollah warned Lebanese authorities against direct negotiations with Israel as Washington pushes forward with a new round of talks between Beirut and Tel Aviv.

item.title: Putin to visit China days after Trump summit as Russia seeks strategic support
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin will meet Xi Jinping in Beijing next week, just after Trump became the first US president to visit China since 2017.

item.title: Israel kills Hamas military chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad in Gaza strike
item.description: An Israeli airstrike killed the head of Hamas armed wing, the most senior Hamas official killed since the October ceasefire. Israel says he helped plan the Oct 7 attacks.

item.title: Unexploded Projectile Found in Romanian Village Near Ukraine Border
item.description: Romanian authorities discovered an unexploded projectile in a village near the Ukraine border, amid ongoing regional tensions from the war.

item.title: One Dead in Comoros as Clashes Erupt Over Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: Protests over fuel price increases turned violent in Comoros, leaving at least one person dead amid economic discontent.

item.title: US in Control of Strait of Hormuz, Trump Says Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: President Trump stated the US, not Iran, controls the Strait of Hormuz as the Iran-Israel conflict continues. Iran has threatened to close the strategic waterway.

item.title: Rural US Voters Support Iran War Despite Economic Pain
item.description: Rural American voters say they support the Iran conflict despite rising fuel prices, viewing the war as necessary for national security.

item.title: Rising Diesel Costs from Iran War Strain US School Budgets
item.description: School districts across the US face budget pressures as diesel prices surge due to the Iran conflict, forcing cuts to transportation services.

item.title: Former Colombian Mayor Killed Weeks Before Presidential Election
item.description: A former mayor allied with presidential candidate Abelardo de La Espriella was shot dead in Colombia, raising concerns about electoral violence ahead of the May 31 vote.

item.title: Iran Moves to Charge Fees for Ships Passing Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran announced plans to charge fees for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions with the US and Israel, potentially disrupting global oil shipments.

item.title: US China Relations at a Crossroads as Trump Visits Beijing
item.description: Trump's visit to China marks the first by a US president in nearly a decade, with discussions focused on Taiwan, trade, and Iran tensions.

item.title: Israel to Sue New York Times Over Sexual Abuse Allegations Against Palestinian Prisoners
item.description: Israel announced plans to sue the New York Times over a report alleging sexual abuse of Palestinian prisoners, claiming the story was fabricated.

item.title: Rwanda Genocide Suspect Kabuga Dies in Custody at 93
item.description: Félicien Kabuga, the Rwandan businessman accused of financing the 1994 genocide, died in custody in The Hague. He was arrested in France in 2020 after 26 years on the run.

item.title: BlackRock Weighs Multibillion-Dollar Investment in SpaceX IPO
item.description: BlackRock is in talks to invest $5-10 billion in SpaceX's initial public offering, signaling strong institutional demand for Elon Musk's space company.

item.title: UK's Starmer Expected to Approve £2.4 Billion Increase in Defense Spending
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to approve a significant defense spending increase as part of efforts to boost military capabilities amid global tensions.

item.title: Eight Dead, 32 Injured After Freight Train Hits Bus in Thailand
item.description: At least eight people were killed and 32 injured when a freight train collided with a bus near Bangkok's airport. The crash caused the bus to burst into flames.

item.title: Nigeria's S&P Ratings Upgraded on Improving Economic Profile
item.description: S&P Global Ratings upgraded Nigeria's credit rating, citing improvements in the country's macroeconomic profile despite regional challenges.

item.title: Gulf Markets Rebound Amid Israel-Iran Conflict
item.description: Stock markets in the Gulf region recovered some losses after being rattled by escalating Israel-Iran tensions in recent sessions.

item.title: US Redirected 78 Ships Near Hormuz During Iran Blockade Operations
item.description: US Central Command reported redirecting 78 commercial vessels near the Strait of Hormuz during Iran's blockade operations amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: Trump Warns Iran of 'Very Bad Time' If No Peace Deal Reached
item.description: President Trump warned Iran of consequences if a peace deal is not reached, as China offered to help negotiate reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
